column1: SAA-C03,"Domain 4: Design Cost-Optimized Architectures","Design Cost-Optimized Architectures","## SAA-C03 Domain 4: Design Cost-Optimized Architectures
This domain covers designing cost-effective compute, storage, database, and network architectures.
- Best for: Batch jobs, data analysis, CI/CD, stateless web servers
- NOT for: Databases, critical applications, stateful workloads
- **Spot Fleet:** Mix of Spot + On-Demand to meet target capacity
- **Spot Block:** Reserved Spot for 1-6 hours (being deprecated)
- **AWS Compute Optimizer:** ML-based recommendations for EC2, EBS, Lambda
- **Cost Explorer:** Right-sizing recommendations based on utilization
- Rule of thumb: If CPU < 40% consistently, downsize
Day 90: Transition to Glacier Instant Retrieval
Day 365: Transition to Glacier Deep Archive
- Can filter by prefix or tags
- Minimum 30 days before transitioning from Standard
- Cannot transition to a higher-cost class

column3: Athena (pay per query) or Redshift Serverless
Avoid cross-AZ charges within same AZ
Cheaper egress than direct + caching reduces origin requests

column1: - NAT Instance (cheaper but self-managed, less HA)
- Tag all resources for cost allocation
- Use AWS Organizations tag policies for consistency
- Common tags: Environment, Team, Project, CostCenter
- Enable Cost Allocation Tags in Billing console
- Use tag-based access control (ABAC) for governance
- **""Steady-state, predictable""** -> Reserved Instances or Savings Plans
- **""Fault-tolerant batch jobs""** -> Spot Instances (up to 90% off)
- **""Mix of reliable + cheap""** -> Spot Fleet (On-Demand base + Spot)
- **""Variable traffic, zero idle cost""** -> Lambda / Fargate
- **""Unknown access patterns""** -> S3 Intelligent-Tiering (no retrieval fees)
- **""Reduce EBS cost""** -> Delete unattached volumes, use gp3
- **""Variable database traffic""** -> Aurora Serverless or DynamoDB On-Demand
- **""Reduce expensive DB reads""** -> ElastiCache or DAX
- **""Infrequent analytics queries""** -> Athena (pay per query scanned)
- **""Reduce data transfer cost""** -> CloudFront (cheaper egress + caching)
- **""Avoid NAT Gateway fees""** -> VPC Gateway Endpoint for S3/DynamoDB
- **""Track spending by team""** -> Cost Allocation Tags + Budgets
- **""Auto-stop idle resources""** -> AWS Budgets actions or Lambda scheduler
- **Savings Plans > Reserved Instances** for flexibility. Compute SP works across instance types/regions.
- **Spot Instances** save up to 90% but can be interrupted. Never for databases or stateful apps.
- **S3 Intelligent-Tiering** = no retrieval fees, auto-moves between tiers. Best for unknown patterns.
- **Glacier Deep Archive** = cheapest storage ($1/TB/month) but 12-48 hour retrieval.
- **VPC Gateway Endpoints** for S3/DynamoDB are free and avoid NAT Gateway data processing fees.
- **CloudFront** has cheaper egress than direct internet transfer AND reduces origin load via caching.
- **Aurora Serverless** can scale to zero ACUs - perfect for dev/test or variable workloads.
- **Athena** = pay per TB scanned. Use columnar formats (Parquet) and partitioning to reduce cost.
- **Lambda** = zero cost when idle. But steady high-throughput is cheaper on EC2 Reserved.
- **Compute Optimizer** uses ML to recommend right-sized instances based on actual utilization.
- **Data transfer between AZs** costs money. Keep tightly coupled services in the same AZ when possible.
- **NAT Gateway** is expensive at scale ($0.045/hr + $0.045/GB). Use VPC Endpoints where possible.
- **Lifecycle policies** automate S3 transitions. Standard -> IA (30 days) -> Glacier (90 days) is common.
- **Spot Fleet** maintains target capacity with a mix of Spot and On-Demand for reliability.
- **Tag everything.** Without tags, you cannot allocate costs to teams or projects.

column2: +-- Reserved: 1-3 year commitment, up to 72% savings
+-- Savings Plans: Flexible commitment, up to 66% (Compute SP)
+-- S3 Classes: Standard > IA > Glacier > Deep Archive
+-- EBS: gp3 over gp2, delete unattached, snapshot lifecycle
+-- Reserved: RDS RI for steady workloads
+-- Caching: ElastiCache/DAX to reduce DB load
+-- VPC Endpoints: Avoid NAT Gateway fees
+-- Direct Connect: Lower per-GB for high volume
+-- Lambda: Zero idle cost, per-request pricing
+-- Fargate: Per vCPU-second, no EC2 management

column1: +-- Spot + On-Demand: Reliable base + cheap burst
+-- Auto Scaling: Match capacity to demand
